_README: Field reference for a TAPPaaS satellite config (ADR-010): ~/config/satellite-<name>.json. A satellite is an EXTERNAL host (kind=external-host), NOT a Proxmox cluster:vm. PRINCIPLE: this file exposes ONLY operator-facing choices; every derived value (tunnel, ports, per-role tuning, backup mechanics, update mode) is defaulted by satellite-manager (lib/provision.sh) — see 'derived' below. Secrets are never stored here.

values.external-host: provisioned on an external host via nixos-anywhere (only value)

values.foundation: foundation-grade but OPTIONAL (not in the mandatory install chain)

name.description: satellite instance name (also the NixOS hostname); a site may run several

values.admin-vpn: blind UDP relay of an admin WireGuard session terminating on OPNsense

dependsOn.note: EMPTY by design — the satellite is operator-driven (satellite-manager), not resolved through install-module.sh. Real prerequisites (network/OPNsense, tappaas-cicd, and backup for the backup role) are checked by satellite-manager.

values.generic: operator supplies a host + SSH; no API automation

values.portal: Tier A (default): operator creates the VPS in the console
values.api: Tier B (opt-in): satellite-manager creates it via the hcloud API token

provider.location.note: Tier B (allocation=api) ONLY — hcloud region for `hcloud server create`. For Tier A you pick it in the console (satellite-manager doesn't use it).

provider.serverType.note: Tier B (allocation=api) ONLY — x86 cx23 default; cax11 = ARM. For Tier A you pick it in the console.

fields._provider_image: dropped — the image is irrelevant (nixos-anywhere kexecs any stock image and reformats to NixOS); satellite-manager defaults it for Tier B.

host.operatorSshKeys.note: standing root SSH key(s) baked into the NixOS satellite (operator out-of-band key; NOT a tappaas-cicd key — §7.3)

backup.s3.endpoint.note: backup role only; operator's S3 endpoint

backup.s3.bucket.note: backup role only; the Object-Lock bucket (must be created with Object Lock enabled)

backup.backend.note: advanced override; 'volume' for a dedicated block volume instead of S3

derived._note: NOT operator config — satellite-manager (lib/provision.sh) computes these with sensible defaults and writes them into the generated satellite-settings.nix. Listed here for reference only.
derived.tunnel: satelliteAddr/homeAddr = a dedicated /31 (default 10.255.0.0/31, auto-allocated per satellite); wgPort 51820 (config knob only if hostile egress needs 443/udp); PersistentKeepalive 25
derived.reverseProxy: forwarder nginx stream; :80 redirect via Caddy; SNI '*' (single-site passthrough); proxyProtocol off until the Caddy side supports it (§2)
derived.adminVpn: terminates on OPNsense (always) into the admin overlay zone; adminWgPort 51821; admin-side MTU ~1340
derived.backup: pull direction; --remove-vanished false; client-side encryption at home; S3 Object-Lock (mode compliance, retention 30d); namespace remote/<name>
derived.update: mode always pull (autoUpgrade from a pinned/signed ref); the cluster never pushes (§7.3)
